channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Ondo begins digital education in 50 secondary schools, trains teachers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ondo State launches digital education in 50 secondary schools, training teachers and students to boost digital literacy and prepare for a technology-driven
Read More: https://punchng.com/ondo-begins-digital-education-in-50-secondary-schools-trains-teachers/

item.title: Lagos releases admission list for model colleges
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Lagos State Government has released the merit admission list for Lagos Model Colleges and Upgraded Secondary Schools 2026. Check your status now!
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-releases-admission-list-for-model-colleges/

item.title: Army arrests five kidnappers in Yobe, recovers weapons
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian Army troops, in collaboration with Yobe Hunters, arrested five kidnappers in Yobe, recovering weapons. This operation is part of ongoing efforts t
Read More: https://punchng.com/army-arrests-five-kidnappers-in-yobe-recovers-weapons/

item.title: Musk predicts AI will outsmart humans within five years
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Elon Musk predicts AI will outsmart humans within five years, ushering in an era of abundance. Learn more about his views on AI’s rapid advancement.
Read More: https://punchng.com/musk-predicts-ai-will-outsmart-humans-within-five-years/

item.title: EPL: Chelsea agree to sign Lacroix from Crystal Palace
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Chelsea have reached an agreement with Crystal Palace to sign defender Maxence Lacroix. Discover how this major transfer strengthens Chelsea’s defence.
Read More: https://punchng.com/epl-chelsea-agree-to-sign-lacroix-from-crystal-palace/

item.title: Throwback clip shows Kenneth Okonkwo previously attacking Atiku
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A throwback clip shows Kenneth Okonkwo, now Atiku Abubakar’s spokesperson, harshly criticising Atiku in a 2023 interview.
Read More: https://punchng.com/throwback-clip-shows-kenneth-okonkwo-previously-attacking-atiku/

item.title: Spain coach says Argentina behaviour 'intolerable' after World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Spain head coach Luis de la Fuente has described the conduct of Argentina players after the World Cup final as "intolerable and unacceptable".

item.title: Pathway Advisors announces launch of Pivot Integrated Energy Services Ltd’s ₦100bn Series 1 Commercial Paper
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Pathway Advisors Limited, the Lead Arranger and Issuing House, is pleased to announce that Pivot Integrated Energy Services Limited (“Pivot” or “the Issuer”) Series 1 Commercial Paper issuance of up to ₦100 billion (“the Offer”) under its ₦300 billion Commercial Paper Programme is NOW OPEN for subsc

item.title: South Sudan: Arms Smuggling - a Key Threat to South Sudan's December Elections
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [ISS] South Sudan's fragile transition is increasingly shaped by forces beyond its borders that drive illicit arms flows into the country.

item.title: Tax ombud, experts seek fairer, transparent tax system to boost compliance
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr Nwabueze said the OTO aims to promote voluntary tax compliance while rebuilding confidence in taxpayers and revenue institutions. The post Tax ombud, experts seek fairer, transparent tax system to boost compliance appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Opinion
item.description: Every generation produces public servants whose influence reaches beyond the offices they occupy. They redefine institutions, challenge entrenched assumptions and

item.title: Momentum Crash Hits YOLO Traders’ Returns by Most in Four Years
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A rapid selloff in the high-flying momentum trade is wrecking the strategy’s staunchest bulls: Retail traders.

item.title: AI Could Surpass Combined Human Intelligence Within 5 Years, Elon Musk Predicts
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk has predicted that artificial intelligence could become more intelligent than the combined capabilities of all humans within the next five years, warning that the technology’s rapid advancement may be impossible to stop. Speaking in an interview with The Economist

item.title: Why I stopped buying gifts for men – Toke Makinwa
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Controversial media personality and actress, Toke Makinwa has revealed that she no longer buys gifts for men she’s romantically involved with.  Speaking in a recent episode of her Toke Moments podcast, which had reality star Beauty Tukura, as a guest, Makinwa said her experience with an o

item.title: Troops arrest five suspected kidnappers, recover arms
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Sector 2, Operation HADIN KAI (OPHK), in conjunction with the Yobe State Hunters Group, have arrested five suspected kidnappers during a well-coordinated, intelligence-led operation. In a statement on Friday, Captain Mohammed Goni, Acting Military Information Officer, Headquarters Joi

item.title: Only 80m Nigerians have access to electricity, says REA
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Isaac Anumihe, Abuja Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer (MD/CEO) of Rural Electrification Agency (REA), Abba Aliyu, has said that about 80 million Nigerians have access to electricity, a situation that calls for more penetration of power to the rural areas. Speaking when a delegation

item.title: Supreme Court Upholds Oyebanji’s APC Governorship Nomination
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Supreme Court has affirmed the nomination of Ekiti State governor Abiodun Oyebanji as the All Progressives Congress (APC) candidate for the June 20 governorship election, dismissing an appeal challenging the party’s primary election. In a unanimous judgment delivered on Friday, a five-member pan

item.title: NANS Announces Commencement of June, July NELFUND Allowance Payments
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) has announced the commencement of payment of June and July upkeep allowances under the Nigerian Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) scheme. NANS National President, Akinteye Babatunde, disclosed this in a statement via his official X handle, on Friday,

item.title: Cholera claims 13 lives in Borno LGA
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Audu said cases of cholera were no longer escalating because of the proactive measures adopted by the Borno Government through the state’s Ministry of Health and the support of the Biu Local Government Chairman, Alhaji Sule Abubakar.

item.title: MTN Foundation Opens Applications for 20th MUSON Scholarship Programme
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: The MTN Foundation has opened applications for the 20th edition of the MTN Foundation - MUSON Music Scholars Programme. This year's edition marks 20 years of the Foundation's partnership with the Musical Society of Nigeria (MUSON), one of Nigeria's longest-running private sector investments in music

item.title: Klopp threatens to quit Germany job over family privacy
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Jurgen Klopp, Germany’s new head coach, warns he will quit if media invades his family’s privacy. Learn more about Klopp’s stance and his new role.

item.description: Cardmember perks are resonating, an Amex executive says. “It’s really about access.”

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Former adult film actress Deyci Alejandra Omaña Ortiz, popularly known by her stage name Amaranta Hank, has officially been sworn in as a senator in Colombia following her victory in the country's March parliamentary elections. item.title: Crystal Palace Eye Nwaiwu As Replacement For Chelsea-Bound Defender
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Crystal Palace have identified Nigerian defender Chibuike Nwaiwu as a potential replacement for Maxence Lacroix, who is on the brink of completing a move to Chelsea, Completesports.com reports. The Eagles have already reached an agreement with Chelsea for the transfer of the French centre-back in a

item.title: Snakebite kills two in Taraba border community as residents decry poor healthcare access
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Two residents of Lissam Sambo (Pangtso) community in Ussa local government council of Taraba State have died from snakebites within 24 hours, raising fresh concerns over the lack of access to life saving medical treatment in the remote border settlement. One of the victims, Elder Bako Awanki, report

item.title: Court seeks proof of Austin Albert’s Dubai surgery, adjourns alleged N460.7m case
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The matter, Inspector General of Police v. Austin Ugochukwu Albert & 2 Others, is being prosecuted by G. T. Nev, Esq. of the Directorate of Legal Services, Nigeria Police Force Headquarters, Abuja, before Hon. Justice I. A. Kala.

item.title: How Nigerian developers can land direct US tech contracts
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The global tech landscape has evolved engineering leads in San Francisco, New York, and Austin are no longer restricting their hiring to local commuter zones. The post How Nigerian developers can land direct US tech contracts appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Spain’s De la Fuente slams Argentina’s World Cup conduct as ‘unacceptable’
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: While Argentina faced criticism through World Cup 2026, post-final altercations are now being investigated.

item.title: Klopp warns he could quit Germany over family privacy concerns
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Germany's newly appointed head coach, Jürgen Klopp, has warned that he will not hesitate to resign if persistent media attention begins to intrude on his family's privacy. The post Klopp warns he could quit Germany over family privacy concerns appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress ADC, Atiku Abubakar, has described former President Olusegun Obasanjo's allegation that he bribed the late Rt. Hon. Ghali Umar Na'Abba to impeach him as politically motivated, aimed at boosting the electoral fortunes of Obasanjo's preferred c

item.description: Former Vice President of Nigeria and presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress, ADC, Atiku Abubakar, has faulted former President Olusegun Obasanjo’s latest allegations against him. In a statement on Friday by his spokesperson, Phrank Shaibu, Atiku said the former Pre

item.title: Whack-a-drone
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: In Pasadena, California, there's a cute red brick courtyard where one storefront isn't like the rest. The glass doors open onto a sparse industrial hallway, which leads to a sunlit foyer with a large spiral staircase. Go up, and you'll see a typical coworking space: open tables, healthy snacks, a co

item.title: Scott: The 'scrawny-looking kid' turned complete midfielder wanted by the elite
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Across the seven games in which Bournemouth took points off Arsenal, Manchester City, Manchester United, Liverpool and Chelsea last season, it is telling that Alex Scott was nearly ever-present, playing 625 of a possible 630 minutes.

item.title: Why do Barcelona want to break transfer records to sign Kerolin?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: It's been brief. Kerolin only joined the Women's Super League from the NWSL 18 months ago. She's about to leave as the English top-flight's most expensive export.

item.title: ‘It’s nothing personal’: Should I tell my 96-year-old stepmother that I don’t want her inheritance?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I would likely be the one responsible for dealing with the house.”

item.title: Africa Corps convoy circumvents sanctions in hazardous new route to Mali
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: A convoy of Russian armoured vehicles taking the route between Lomé and Bamako, passing through Burkina Faso, shows that Africa Corps has found a workaround after Guinea refused to admit a sanctioned cargo ship.

item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: Chude Jideonwo released the first episode of his three-part series “Madam President,” and before you catch up on it, we have compiled a list of characters we believe you should keep an eye on when you start the series. These characters we’ve listed below played their roles perfectly and kept us glue

item.title: EFCC Arraigns Man Over Alleged N3.8m Property Fraud In Maiduguri
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Maiduguri Zonal Directorate of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has arraigned one Alhaji Muhammad Talake before the Borno State High Court sitting in Maiduguri over an alleged N3.8 million property fraud. Talake was arraigned on Thursday before Justice Aisha Kumaliya on a two-

item.title: US-Saudi sign nuclear deal, then Trump introduces new condition
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Abraham Accord is a series of agreements brokered under Mr Trump’s first administration, which led several Arab countries to normalise diplomatic relations with Israel. The post US-Saudi sign nuclear deal, then Trump introduces new condition appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Amidst Benue Insecurity, IGP’s visit, Governor Alia renews call for state police
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “The conversation should no longer be about whether Nigeria needs state police but how to make it operational,” Governor Hyacinth Alia said during a security meeting with the Inspector-General of Police in Makurdi. item.title: Anambra Govt To Shut Down Private, Public Schools Over Graduation Ceremonies
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Private and public schools in Anambra State that failed to comply with the State government ban on graduation ceremonies would be shut down. However graduation ceremonies for students graduating from Senior Secondary Schools would be allowed but must be volunteering without any form of payment. Acco

item.title: Peter Obi reacts to Enugu Air runway incident in Benin
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: PREMIUM TIMES earlier reported that an Enugu Air Embraer E170, operating Flight 4264 from Lagos, veered off the runway after landing at Benin Airport on Thursday. The post Peter Obi reacts to Enugu Air runway incident in Benin appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: CAF Confirms 24-Team Format for 2027 Africa Cup of Nations
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has confirmed that the 2027 TotalEnergies Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) will feature 24 national teams, dismissing reports suggesting a possible change to the tournament format. CAF President Dr Patrice Motsepe made the clarification during a press confere

item.title: How crystal meth addiction drove me to pull out my teeth – DJ Fat Tony
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “At the time, my face was always numb and I just wanted them out of my mouth,” he said. “I was so mentally unwell that I thought it was normal. I would sit rocking backwards and forwards to stabilise myself.”

item.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The United States has condemned the killing of members of the family of Plateau-based cleric, Rev. Ezekiel Dachomo, calling for those responsible for the attack to be brought to justice and urging the Nigerian government to strengthen security in vulnerable communities. item.description: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: One person has been confirmed dead and at least two others injured following multiple road crashes along the Kara Bridge section of the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway, leading to a massive traffic gridlock that stranded hundreds of commuters and disrupted economic activities on Friday. item.title: AfCFTA chief Wamkele Mene: ‘Africa cannot prosper while it remains fragmented’
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: With 50 countries signed up but intra-African trade still stuck at about 15%, Mene says lower barriers, cheaper logistics and freer movement are vital to building a single African market.

item.description: Nigeria's Super Falcons will begin their quest for a record-extending 11th Women's Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) title when they face Malawi in their opening Group C fixture on Sunday in Morocco. The court refused, questioned whether the prosecution had become personal and raised the spectre of a w

item.title: Germany Appoint Klopp As National Team Coach
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Having repeatedly said his time in the dugout was over, Klopp returns with the mammoth task of returning the struggling Germans to the top of the international game. The post Germany Appoint Klopp As National Team Coach appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Reps Pass Bill For Federal College Of Fisheries Technology Rivers
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives has passed the bill to establish Federal College Of Fisheries Technology & Marine Engineering, NGO, Rivers State. This followed the consideration and adoption of the report presented by the chairman, House Committee on Ocean and Fisheries, Hon. Akarachi Amadi at the

item.title: Lagos Releases Admission List For Model Colleges, Upgraded Secondary Schools
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Lagos State Ministry of Basic and Secondary Education has released the merit admission list for candidates seeking admission into the state's Model Colleges and Upgraded Secondary Schools. The post Lagos Releases Admission List For Model Colleges, Upgraded Secondary Schools appeared first on Tre

item.title: Kogi Political Forum commends Tinubu Over Appointment Of Abuh Mohammed As NPC DG
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A political group, the Kogi State Political Elite Forum, has commended President Bola Tinubu over the appointment of Dr. Abuh Mohammed as Director-General of the National Population Commission (NPC). The group also acknowledged the appointments of Prof. Ronke Bello as a member of the National

item.title: Civil War: Convenient Amnesia – Ulasi accuses Gowon of distorting history in memoir
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A chieftain of the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, Dan Ulasi, has criticised former Head of State, General Yakubu Gowon (retd.), over his recently published memoir on the Nigerian Civil War, describing parts of the account as “convenient amnesia” and “half-truths.” Speaking on The Morning Show on Ari

item.title: “I’m an African”: Why MTN says migration matters to South Africa’s digital future
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: For a company that has built its growth on connecting Africans across borders, those developments have become impossible to ignore. Mupita deliberately framed his own migration story as part of MTN's identity.

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Saadia Zahidi is a global economist and policy expert who has spent more than two decades shaping international conversations on the future of work, economic growth and workforce transformation. item.title: 'Like a movie playing in my head' | Klopp honoured to take Germany job
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Jurgen Klopp admits he is still coming to terms with being confirmed as the new head coach of the German national team, describing it as 'like a movie in my head'.

item.title: South-West CAN urges Tinubu to withhold assent to FRSC amendment bill
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Following the proposed amendment to the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) Bill, which seeks to criminalise preaching in commercial vehicles, the South-West chapter of the Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN) has urged President Bola Tinubu to withhold assent to the legislation, describing the provi
